metadata.title: Connect to Azure Stack | Microsoft Docs
metadata.description: Learn how to connect Azure Stack.
metadata.author: mattbriggs
metadata.ms.author: mabrigg

content.markdown: To manage resources, you must connect to the Azure Stack Development Kit. This article details the steps required to connect to the development kit. You can use either of the following connection options:
content.text: To manage resources, you must connect to the Azure Stack Development Kit. This article details the steps required to connect to the development kit. You can use either of the following connection options:

content.markdown: To manage resources, you must connect to the Azure Stack Development Kit. This article details the steps required to connect to the development kit. You can use either of the following connection options:
content.text: To manage resources, you must connect to the Azure Stack Development Kit. This article details the steps required to connect to the development kit. You can use either of the following connection options:
Remote Desktop: lets a single concurrent user quickly connect from the development kit.

content.markdown: Remote Desktop: lets a single concurrent user quickly connect from the development kit.
content.text: Remote Desktop: lets a single concurrent user quickly connect from the development kit.
Virtual Private Network (VPN): lets multiple concurrent users connect from clients outside of the Azure Stack infrastructure (requires configuration).

content.markdown: Virtual Private Network (VPN): lets multiple concurrent users connect from clients outside of the Azure Stack infrastructure (requires configuration).
content.text: Virtual Private Network (VPN): lets multiple concurrent users connect from clients outside of the Azure Stack infrastructure (requires configuration).

content.title: Connect to Azure Stack with Remote Desktop

content.markdown: With a Remote Desktop Connection, a single concurrent user can work with the portal to manage resources.
content.text: With a Remote Desktop Connection, a single concurrent user can work with the portal to manage resources.

content.markdown: With a Remote Desktop Connection, a single concurrent user can work with the portal to manage resources.
content.text: With a Remote Desktop Connection, a single concurrent user can work with the portal to manage resources.
Open a Remote Desktop Connection and connect to the development kit. Enter **AzureStack\AzureStackAdmin** as the username, and the administrative password that you provided during Azure Stack setup.

content.markdown: Open a Remote Desktop Connection and connect to the development kit. Enter
content.text: Open a Remote Desktop Connection and connect to the development kit. Enter
AzureStack\AzureStackAdmin
AzureStack\AzureStackAdmin

content.markdown: as the username, and the administrative password that you provided during Azure Stack setup.
content.text: as the username, and the administrative password that you provided during Azure Stack setup.
From the development kit computer, open Server Manager, click **Local Server**, turn off Internet Explorer Enhanced Security, and then close Server Manager.

content.markdown: From the development kit computer, open Server Manager, click
content.text: From the development kit computer, open Server Manager, click
Local Server
Local Server

content.markdown: , turn off Internet Explorer Enhanced Security, and then close Server Manager.
content.text: , turn off Internet Explorer Enhanced Security, and then close Server Manager.
To open the portal, go to (https://portal.local.azurestack.external/) and sign in using user credentials.

content.markdown: To open the portal, go to (https://portal.local.azurestack.external/) and sign in using user credentials.
content.text: To open the portal, go to (https://portal.local.azurestack.external/) and sign in using user credentials.

content.title: Connect to Azure Stack with VPN

content.markdown: You can establish a split tunnel VPN connection to an Azure Stack Development Kit. Through the VPN connection you can access the admin portal, user portal, and locally installed tools such as Visual Studio and PowerShell to manage Azure Stack resources. VPN connectivity is supported in both Azure Active Directory (AAD) and Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS)-based deployments. VPN connections let multiple clients connect to Azure Stack at the same time.
content.text: You can establish a split tunnel VPN connection to an Azure Stack Development Kit. Through the VPN connection you can access the admin portal, user portal, and locally installed tools such as Visual Studio and PowerShell to manage Azure Stack resources. VPN connectivity is supported in both Azure Active Directory (AAD) and Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS)-based deployments. VPN connections let multiple clients connect to Azure Stack at the same time.

content.markdown: You can establish a split tunnel VPN connection to an Azure Stack Development Kit. Through the VPN connection you can access the admin portal, user portal, and locally installed tools such as Visual Studio and PowerShell to manage Azure Stack resources. VPN connectivity is supported in both Azure Active Directory (AAD) and Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS)-based deployments. VPN connections let multiple clients connect to Azure Stack at the same time.
content.text: You can establish a split tunnel VPN connection to an Azure Stack Development Kit. Through the VPN connection you can access the admin portal, user portal, and locally installed tools such as Visual Studio and PowerShell to manage Azure Stack resources. VPN connectivity is supported in both Azure Active Directory (AAD) and Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS)-based deployments. VPN connections let multiple clients connect to Azure Stack at the same time.

content.markdown:
[!NOTE] 
This VPN connection doesn't provide connectivity to Azure Stack infrastructure VMs.
content.text:
[!NOTE] 
This VPN connection doesn't provide connectivity to Azure Stack infrastructure VMs.
[!NOTE]

content.markdown: This VPN connection doesn't provide connectivity to Azure Stack infrastructure VMs.
content.text: This VPN connection doesn't provide connectivity to Azure Stack infrastructure VMs.
Prerequisites
Prerequisites
Install [Azure Stack compatible Azure PowerShell](../operator/azure-stack-powershell-install.md) on your local computer.
Install
Azure Stack compatible Azure PowerShell
on your local computer.
Download the [tools required to work with Azure Stack](../operator/azure-stack-powershell-download.md).
Download the
tools required to work with Azure Stack
Configure VPN connectivity
Configure VPN connectivity

content.markdown: To create a VPN connection to the development kit, open an elevated PowerShell session from your local Windows-based computer and run the following script (make sure to update the IP address and password values for your environment):
content.text: To create a VPN connection to the development kit, open an elevated PowerShell session from your local Windows-based computer and run the following script (make sure to update the IP address and password values for your environment):

content.markdown: To create a VPN connection to the development kit, open an elevated PowerShell session from your local Windows-based computer and run the following script (make sure to update the IP address and password values for your environment):
content.text: To create a VPN connection to the development kit, open an elevated PowerShell session from your local Windows-based computer and run the following script (make sure to update the IP address and password values for your environment):

content.markdown:
```powershell
# Configure winrm if it's not already configured
winrm quickconfig  

Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned

# Import the Connect module
Import-Module .\Connect\AzureStack.Connect.psm1 

# Add the development kit computer's host IP address & certificate authority (CA) to the list of trusted hosts. Make sure to update the IP address and password values for your environment. 

$hostIP = "<Azure Stack host IP address>"

$Password = ConvertTo-SecureString `
  "<Administrator password provided when deploying Azure Stack>" `
  -AsPlainText `
  -Force

Set-Item wsman:\localhost\Client\TrustedHosts `
  -Value $hostIP `
  -Concatenate

# Create a VPN connection entry for the local user
Add-AzsVpnConnection `
  -ServerAddress $hostIP `
  -Password $Password```
content.code:
# Configure winrm if it's not already configured
winrm quickconfig  

Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned

# Import the Connect module
Import-Module .\Connect\AzureStack.Connect.psm1 

# Add the development kit computer's host IP address & certificate authority (CA) to the list of trusted hosts. Make sure to update the IP address and password values for your environment. 

$hostIP = "<Azure Stack host IP address>"

$Password = ConvertTo-SecureString `
  "<Administrator password provided when deploying Azure Stack>" `
  -AsPlainText `
  -Force

Set-Item wsman:\localhost\Client\TrustedHosts `
  -Value $hostIP `
  -Concatenate

# Create a VPN connection entry for the local user
Add-AzsVpnConnection `
  -ServerAddress $hostIP `
  -Password $Password

content.markdown: If the setup succeeds, you'll see `azurestack` in your list of VPN connections.
content.text: If the setup succeeds, you'll see `azurestack` in your list of VPN connections.

Connect to Azure Stack

content.markdown: Connect to the Azure Stack instance by using either of the following two methods:
content.text: Connect to the Azure Stack instance by using either of the following two methods:

content.markdown: Connect to the Azure Stack instance by using either of the following two methods:
content.text: Connect to the Azure Stack instance by using either of the following two methods:

When prompted, trust the Azure Stack host and install the certificate from **AzureStackCertificateAuthority** onto your local computer's certificate store. The prompt might appear behind the PowerShell session window.

content.markdown: When prompted, trust the Azure Stack host and install the certificate from
content.text: When prompted, trust the Azure Stack host and install the certificate from
AzureStackCertificateAuthority
AzureStackCertificateAuthority

content.markdown: onto your local computer's certificate store. The prompt might appear behind the PowerShell session window.
content.text: onto your local computer's certificate store. The prompt might appear behind the PowerShell session window.
On your local computer, go to **Network Settings** > **VPN** > select `azurestack` > **connect**. At the sign-in prompt, enter the username (AzureStack\AzureStackAdmin) and the password.

content.markdown: On your local computer, go to
content.text: On your local computer, go to
Network Settings
Network Settings
VPN
VPN
> select
azurestack
connect
connect

content.markdown: . At the sign-in prompt, enter the username (AzureStack\AzureStackAdmin) and the password.
content.text: . At the sign-in prompt, enter the username (AzureStack\AzureStackAdmin) and the password.
Test the VPN connectivity
Test the VPN connectivity

content.markdown: To test the portal connection, open a browser and go to the user portal (https://portal.local.azurestack.external/), sign in, and then create resources.
content.text: To test the portal connection, open a browser and go to the user portal (https://portal.local.azurestack.external/), sign in, and then create resources.
